frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment properly positioned for extracorporeal suturing task

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle positioned appropriately in needle holder

frame_analyses.description: Hands manipulating needle and suture around circular marked area
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture being positioned through marked points on material

frame_analyses.description: Continued manipulation of suture through material with both hands
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady hand positioning, suture appears to pass through appropriate location

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot structure appears secure before pushing

frame_analyses.description: Hands working to advance knot down to tissue level

frame_analyses.description: Continued knot advancement with visible suture tension
Final positioning and preparation for suture cutting
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears seated properly at tissue level

penalties.description: Minor deviation from optimal suture placement through marked points

confidence_rationale: Clear visualization of most phases and technique. Some uncertainty in exact millimeter measurements of suture deviation from marked points due to camera angle and resolution. Completion time and knot formation well-observed.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation measurements at entry/exit points - estimated from visual assessment
Final suture cutting phase not visible in provided frames
technique_summary: Solid intermediate performance with good extracorporeal knot technique. Efficient needle loading and knot formation phases. Total time of 90 seconds places performance in intermediate range.
strengths: Efficient needle loading and initial setup
Appropriate tension maintenance during knot pushing
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise suture placement through marked points to minimize deviation penalties
Practice to reduce overall time toward expert benchmark of 60-100 seconds
